task_completion.description: Verify that cert-manager was installed using the provided manifest.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the execution of 'kubectl apply -f /home/ga/Desktop/cert-manager.yaml' with 'created'/'configured' outputs, or the Rancher UI showing active pods in the 'cert-manager' namespace.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'local-selfsigned' ClusterIssuer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output of 'kubectl get clusterissuer' or 'kubectl describe clusterissuer local-selfsigned' showing the issuer exists and has the type 'selfSigned', or the equivalent configuration visible in the Rancher UI.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'shop-ingress' Ingress in the 'e-commerce' namespace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output (e.g., 'kubectl get ingress -n e-commerce') or Rancher UI showing 'shop-ingress' configured to route the host 'shop.example.com' to the 'shop-frontend' service on port 80.

task_completion.description: Verify the Ingress is annotated for cert-manager and configured with the correct TLS secret.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output of 'kubectl describe ingress shop-ingress -n e-commerce' or Rancher UI showing the annotation 'cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: local-selfsigned' and a TLS block referencing the secret 'shop-tls-secret'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Certificate and Secret were successfully generated by cert-manager.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing 'kubectl get certificate -n e-commerce' with a READY status, or 'kubectl get secret shop-tls-secret -n e-commerce' showing the auto-provisioned TLS secret.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent logged into Rancher.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser window open to 'https://localhost' displaying the authenticated Rancher dashboard/UI.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually applied the configurations to the cluster.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for executed commands in the terminal (like 'kubectl apply') or 'Save'/'Create' actions in the Rancher UI, rather than just YAML files open in a text editor without being applied.

integrity.description: Ensure verification outputs are from the actual Kubernetes cluster.
integrity.visual_evidence: Outputs from 'kubectl get' or 'kubectl describe' should contain genuine cluster-generated fields (like creation timestamps, UIDs, and status conditions) and not appear manually typed or mocked.

integrity.description: Ensure the TLS secret was auto-provisioned by cert-manager, not manually created.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check terminal history to ensure the agent did not run 'kubectl create secret tls shop-tls-secret'. The secret should appear as a result of the Ingress annotation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Namespace: e-commerce. Manifest Path: /home/ga/Desktop/cert-manager.yaml. Issuer Name: local-selfsigned. Ingress Name: shop-ingress. Target Host: shop.example.com. Target Secret: shop-tls-secret.
